alma
  • Home
  • App
  • Support
  • Download

History

Conversations persist locally. The feature that broke every E2E test after reload.

How it works

All conversations are stored in localStorage on the web. The sidebar shows a list of conversations with titles and timestamps. You can search, rename, delete, and switch between them.

The alma_active_conversation key tracks which conversation is currently selected. When you reload the page, the app checks this key and restores the last conversation.

The localStorage problem

Every E2E test reloads the page at some point. When the page reloads, the app reads alma_active_conversation from localStorage. If a conversation was selected before reload, the app enters "restoring" mode. It loads the conversation and renders the conversation view instead of the landing page.

The landing page shows suggestions. The conversation view does not show suggestions. The E2E tests expected suggestions after reload. They got a conversation view instead.

The fix: clear alma_active_conversation from localStorage before every reload in the E2E tests.

# Before: just reload
page.reload()

# After: clear active conversation first
page.evaluate("localStorage.removeItem('alma_active_conversation')")
page.reload()

Search in sidebar

The sidebar has a search bar. It filters conversations by title. The filter is case-insensitive. The Swift app implements this with a computed property:

private var filteredConversations: [ConversationIndexEntry] {
    if searchText.isEmpty {
        return service.conversations
    }
    return service.conversations.filter {
        $0.title.localizedCaseInsensitiveContains(searchText)
    }
}

Rename and delete

Both use native alerts in the Swift app. The rename alert has a text field pre-filled with the current title. The delete alert shows a destructive confirmation with "This cannot be undone."

On the web, both use custom modals. The delete modal has a confirmation checkbox. The rename modal has a text input.

The macOS app

The Swift app persists conversations through the Flask backend, not localStorage. The ConversationService loads conversations from the API on launch. It saves the selected conversation ID to UserDefaults. On relaunch, it restores the last conversation.

private var savedConversationId: String? {
    get { defaults.string(forKey: selectedIdKey) }
    set { defaults.set(newValue, forKey: selectedIdKey) }
}

The web uses localStorage. The Swift app uses UserDefaults. Both persist the same data through different mechanisms.

What broke in E2E

  • Suggestions missing after reload, alma_active_conversation in localStorage caused restoring mode. Fix: clear it before reload.
  • Wrong conversation loaded, the test created a conversation, reloaded, and expected a different conversation to be selected. Fix: clear localStorage before each test.
  • Sidebar not visible, the sidebar was collapsed on mobile viewport. Fix: set viewport to 1280x720 before sidebar tests.

Found something unexpected? Report an issue.
